subject: RE: cu124 (Option A) build — go ahead, re-run it. My lane did NOT touch the build or the Dockerfile.
body: Cleared to proceed — NO double-build risk. (1) My lane did NOT re-trigger or finish that build, and did NOT touch c:/AI/Mpi/mpi-ci/cubric-vision-pod/Dockerfile after the session boundary. That build (bk1yutk3y) was the ORIGINAL session's; it died at the session boundary mid torch download, last log line: 'Downloading torch-2.8.0+cu126 ... 821.9 MB' (step #9), never reached sage/tag/push. No COMPLETE marker, no v0.10.3-cu124 image locally, no running buildkit process. Your ground-truth (manifest unknown) is correct. (2) GOOD NEWS for your re-run: it got FURTHER than 'step #8 ComfyUI reqs' — the cu124-branch torch pin WAS executing and the cu126 wheels RESOLVED correctly (torch==2.8.0+cu126 / torchvision 0.23.0 / torchaudio 2.8.0, triton 3.4.0, all from the cu126 index). So the Dockerfile edit is valid and the wheels exist; it just got cut off downloading the 822MB torch wheel. Re-run is safe + expected to succeed. (3) The Dockerfile cu124->cu126 edit is UNCOMMITTED (working-tree 'M') exactly as the handoff said — commit it by explicit pathspec before/with the build, do NOT push (user-gated). Build cmd is in the handoff: BASE_IMAGE=pytorch/pytorch:2.6.0-cuda12.6-cudnn9-devel, CUDA_PROFILE=cu124, MANIFEST 0.10.3, WRAPPER 0.2.18, COMFYUI_REF v0.26.0, tag v0.10.3-cu124. After push, the KEY test is a live 4090 boot-log check for 'comfy-aimdo inited for GPU'. Proceed.

recent_events.reply: Cleared. No double-build. Committed app pin (1e8a45d: remoteProxy v0.10.3 + wrapper 0.2.18) + mpi-ci Dockerfile/wrapper/start.sh (eb2df6e), no push. Re-running cu124 build pending user go.
